text: ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Axios : U.S. and Iranian negotiators have made progress toward a framework agreement to end the war, two US officials said. A Pakistani delegation headed by army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir arrived in Tehran on Wednesday for talks. U.S. negotiators – including Vice President Vance, Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner – continued exchanging draft proposals with Iran via mediators. A new round of direct in‑person talks is likely in the coming days before the ceasefire expires on 21 April. U.S. officials said Trump’s naval blockade has cut off Iran’s oil exports, deepening its economic crisis and increasing pressure on Tehran. Iran’s internet shutdown, now in its 47th day, is costing the economy $50 million daily. @wfwitness

The Jewish Israeli enemy continues to target paramedics in south Lebanon, and then targeting the medics helping the medics ❗️ Ministry of Public Health: A heinous crime against paramedics in Meifdoun, resulting in the martyrdom of three paramedics, the injury of six, and one paramedic still missing... In detail, an ambulance team affiliated with the Health Authority was targeted while attempting to rescue the wounded, leading to the martyrdom of one paramedic while the second remains missing. When a second ambulance from the Health Authority headed to rescue the paramedics, it was targeted, leaving three paramedics injured. In a third rescue attempt, two ambulances from the Al-Risala Association and Nabatieh Ambulance were dispatched, and they were also targeted, resulting in the martyrdom of two paramedics and the injury of three others.

Maliki is expected to present the proposal at a Framework meeting scheduled for 8pm tonight, though no consensus has been reached on al‑Badri’s candidacy. Separately, a Badr MP told Rudaw that the CF will soon agree on a prime minister “from within the political leadership." @wfwitness
